<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******.>> <<Wrap every word>> <<bigram in double>> <<angular brackets,>> <<such as <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>.>> <<There should be>> <<6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and>> <<only paragraphs are>> <<separated with each>> <<other by two new>> <<lines as if it was>> <<'\n\n' in python.>> <<Paragraph 5 must>> <<start with word team.>> <<Include keyword>> <<possession in your>> <<response.

<<The function will>> <<traverse through>> <<each element in>> <<the list, checking>> <<if it is a tuple.>> <<If it is, it will>> <<recursively process>> <<its elements to>> <<extract all numeric>> <<values. Non-numeric>> <<entities will be>> <<ignored during this>> <<process. The cumulative>> <<sum will be calculated>> <<by adding all these>> <<numeric values. This>> <<approach ensures that>> <<even deeply nested>> <<tuples are handled>> <<efficiently and correctly.>> <<The possession of>> <<the function is to>> <<extract and sum>> <<numerical data from>> <<complex structures.>> <<This makes it a>> <<versatile tool for>> <<data analysis tasks.>>

<<Another approach>> <<to the same problem>> <<is to use a helper>> <<function that checks>> <<each element in the>> <<structure for being>> <<a tuple or a number.>> <<If it is a tuple, the>> <<helper will call>> <<itself recursively.>> <<If it is a number, it>> <<will be added to the>> <<sum. This method>> <<is efficient and>> <<easy to understand.>> <<It ensures that all>> <<numeric values are>> <<collected and summed>> <<without any issues.>> <<The possession of>> <<this method is that>> <<it maintains clarity>> <<
